channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Toronto Street Festival Shooting Sparks Calls for Transparency and Safety Measures
item.description: A shooting incident at the Salsa on St. Clair street festival in Toronto has left two people dead and five injured, sparking calls for greater transparency from police and discussions about public saf...

item.title: Government's Expanded 4PH Program Delivers 8,000 Homes to Filipino Families
item.description: The Department of Human Settlements and Urban Development (DHSUD) and the Pag-IBIG Fund have successfully turned over 8,000 housing units under the Expanded 4PH Program in Tarlac, marking significant ...

item.title: Burundi and Mali's Authoritarian Tactics: Blaming External Enemies to Sustain Power
item.description: The article explores how Burundi and Mali, both authoritarian regimes, employ a shared strategy of blaming external enemies to consolidate power. Burundi's President Évariste Ndayishimiye visited Burk...

item.title: Australia Introduces New AI Regulations to Balance Innovation and Protection
item.description: The Australian government has announced plans to establish AI standards and a dedicated AI office by 2027, aiming to balance technological advancement with safeguards for households, workers, and crea...

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup quarterfinal fixtures announced
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has advanced to the quarterfinal stage, with the last-eight matches scheduled across multiple locations in the United States. The fixtures, listed in South African time, includ...

item.title: Michael Steele Criticizes Todd Blanche's Testimony on Judicial War Comments
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche faced intense scrutiny during a House Appropriations Subcommittee hearing, where former Republican Party chairman Michael Steele accused him of misleading lawmaker...

item.title: England Lose World Cup Semi-Final to Argentina After Tuchel's Defensive Substitutions Lead to Late Comeback
item.description: In a dramatic World Cup semi-final, England suffered a 2-1 defeat to Argentina after a series of controversial defensive substitutions by manager Thomas Tuchel. The Three Lions took an early lead thro...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi's Campaign and CJP Protest Developments
item.description: The article discusses Rahul Gandhi's absence from the CJP-Wangchuk protest and his upcoming campaign events. Gandhi is set to hold a rally in Dehradun on July 17 as part of his 'Chhatron Ki Chhat' ini...

item.title: Cartrack employee's death under investigation as Labour Department probes workplace conditions
item.description: The Labour Department's investigation into the death of Cartrack employee Gcina Dhladhla has intensified, with multiple parties involved. The department initially found no issues during an annual insp...

item.title: Zanzibar's Tourism Sector Relies Heavily on European Visitors
item.description: Tourism statistics reveal that Europe dominates visitor arrivals to Zanzibar, accounting for 62% of all tourists. The semi-autonomous region of Tanzania, known for its pristine beaches and cultural he...

item.title: Argentina advances to World Cup final with late goal against England
item.description: England's hopes of reaching the FIFA World Cup final were dashed in a dramatic semifinal against Argentina, which ended in a 2-1 loss for the Three Lions. The match, held in Atlanta, saw England take ...

item.title: How Sports Science is Extending Soccer Players' Careers

item.title: UN agency highlights 25% increase in healthy diet costs since 2021
item.description: A UN agency has issued a warning about the significant rise in the cost of maintaining a healthy diet, noting a 25% increase since 2021. This surge in expenses has raised concerns about the affordabil...

item.title: UK-Argentina trade ties amid 2026 World Cup preparations
item.description: The article discusses the evolving trade relationship between the United Kingdom and Argentina in the context of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It highlights how the event is serving as a platform for bilat...

item.title: Argentina's Dramatic Comeback Against England Secures World Cup Final Spot
item.description: In a thrilling semi-final clash at the Atlanta Stadium, Argentina staged a remarkable 2-1 comeback against England to secure their place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final. The match, which drew a crowd...

item.title: Tom Cruise to Appear in FIFA World Cup 2026 Closing Ceremony
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 has confirmed that actor Tom Cruise will participate in the star-studded closing ceremony of the tournament, which will take place on July 19 at the New York New Jersey Stadium...

item.title: England and Argentina Draw 0-0 in High-Octane World Cup Semifinal
item.description: In a fiercely contested World Cup semifinal, England and Argentina played out a 0-0 draw after 90 minutes of intense, physical football. The match, held in 2026, showcased a blend of tactical battles ...

item.title: DepEd's Active Shooter Drills: Guidelines for Students and Staff
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has mandated active threat drills for all schools, emphasizing safety protocols during emergencies. The drill at Manila Science High School highlighted three key ac...

item.title: Messi leads Argentina's thrilling comeback victory over England in World Cup semi-final
item.description: Lionel Messi delivered a masterclass performance, providing two assists to help Argentina stage a remarkable comeback against England in their 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-final. The match, held at Atlant...

item.title: Italian airline Neos starts direct flights to Tanzania, boosting tourism
item.description: Italian airline Neos has launched direct flights to Tanzania, marking a significant boost for the tourism sector. The maiden flight landed at Kilimanjaro International Airport (KIA) on July 15, 2026, ...
